key_insight: The paper introduces a fully open-weight multilingual pipeline that turns unstructured news text into signed, temporal political-elite knowledge graphs by jointly extracting entities and relationships across languages.
engineering_relevance: It offers a scalable, reproducible workflow for building structured relational datasets from messy multilingual corpora, reducing manual coding while improving cross-country comparability.
method: Span-based NER to detect person, organization, and other relevant mentions
Three-stage entity linking cascade to map mentions to language-independent Wikidata IDs
Ontology-constrained mixture-of-experts model with guided decoding for directed, signed relation extraction
evidence_signal: A full-coverage spot-check against 3,491 gold-standard relations reported 68.2% strict correctness and 93.7% lenient correctness, with case studies in Austria and Poland matching the public record.
watch_item: Performance depends on high-quality ontology design and entity resolution, and relation extraction accuracy may still drop in ambiguous or low-resource languages.
paper_title: Mapping Political-Elite Networks in Europe with a Multilingual Joint Entity-Relation Extraction Pipeline
